name: AMERICAN BATTERY TECHNOLOGY Co
business: American Battery Technology Company is an integrated critical battery materials company that develops technologies for extracting battery metals from primary resources and recycling lithium-ion batteries. It sells recycled battery materials such as black mass and metal byproducts, and plans to sell battery-grade lithium hydroxide. Its customers include cathode manufacturers, battery cell manufacturers, and OEMs in the electric vehicle, stationary storage, and consumer electronics industries.

exposures.sentence: Lithium-ion batteries have become the rechargeable battery of choice in cell phones, computers, electric vehicles, and large scale electric stationary storage systems.
Lithium-ion batteries have become the rechargeable battery of choice in cell phones, computers, electric vehicles, and large scale electric stationary storage systems.

The Company ... through its engagement in the exploration of new primary resources of battery metals, the development and commercialization of new technologies for the extraction of these battery metals from primary resources.

Safety concerns in handling lithium-ion batteries, changes in battery chemistry or technology, slower-than-expected adoption of electric vehicles or stationary energy storage batteries, or reduced government support for clean energy could all negatively impact our revenues and operating results.

The Company ... commercialization of an internally developed integrated process for the recycling of lithium-ion batteries ... to extract lithium, nickel, cobalt, manganese and other products and upgrade them to the battery cathode grade specifications.

catalysts.event: Selected for $19.5 million tax credit under 48C program for battery recycling facility
catalysts.sentence: On March 28, 2024, the Company was selected for an approximately $19.5 million tax credit through the Qualifying Advanced Energy Project Credits program (the 48C program).

catalysts.event: Selected for $40.5 million tax credit under 48C program for next-generation commercial battery recycling facility
catalysts.sentence: Also on March 28, 2024, the Company has been selected for an additional $40.5 million tax credit through the 48C program to support the design and construction of a new, next-generation, commercial battery recycling facility to be located in the United States.

catalysts.event: Received Letter of Interest for up to $900 million debt financing from US Export-Import Bank for Tonopah Flats Lithium Project
catalysts.sentence: On April 23, 2025, ABTC received a Letter of Interest from the US Export-Import Bank for up to $900 million in low-interest debt financing to support the construction of the Tonopah Flats Lithium Project.

catalysts.event: Completed construction and commissioning of lithium hydroxide pilot plant
catalysts.sentence: The Company has completed the construction and commissioning of its lithium hydroxide (LiOH) pilot plant, marking a significant milestone in the commercialization of its internally-developed processes to access an unrealized domestic primary lithium resource.

catalysts.event: Completed contractual requirements of $0.5 million USABC grant
catalysts.sentence: The Company and its partners successfully completed all contractual requirements of its $0.5 million grant award from the US Advanced Battery Consortium, which is comprised of the US DOE, General Motors, Ford Motor Company, and Stellantis NV, including the recycling of commercial quantities of batteries, the purification and manufacturing of battery grade precursors, the manufacturing of high energy density cathode active material, and the manufacturing and testing of approximately 100 automotive scale multi-layer pouch cell batteries.

tamClaims.claim: Global lithium-ion battery market surpassed $100B in 2024 and is projected to exceed $250B by 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: The global market for lithium-ion batteries surpassed $100B in 2024 and is projected to exceed $250B by 2030, as there continues to be technology, regulatory, and social movement driving demand growth.
